item.title: Post-hoc Telegram monitor — host-side secret scanner the agent cannot disable
item.intent: Even if the egress proxy is somehow bypassed (cert issue, proxy crash), a host-side monitor must independently scan outbound Telegram messages and alert on secret detection. Defense-in-depth requires layers the agent cannot control.
item.description: Clawdius pattern: a cron-driven Python script that polls Telegram getUpdates, filters for bot-sent messages, and scans them for secret patterns. Runs on the host (not in container) so the agent cannot disable it. Alerts via email on detection. Reference: clawdius/scripts/telegram-egress-monitor.py.
steps: Generate cron schedule (every 5 min during configured waking hours)
Scan for: env var literal values, base64 variants, token regex patterns
Alert via configured notification channel on detection
Add doctor check: monitor running, last run recent
acceptanceCriteria: Host-side monitor script is generated for Telegram-enabled deployments
Monitor scans bot-sent messages for secret patterns
Alerts fire on detection via configured notification channel
Monitor runs independently of the agent container
